title: Major US aluminum producer boosts production to full capacity
summary: This video report says a major U.S. aluminum producer is boosting production to full capacity amid tariff-related trade moves. It also notes Century Aluminum’s decision to expand output and references job creation tied to the move.

title: New Report: U.S. Primary Aluminum Demand Projected to Surge by up to 40% by 2035 Amid Trade and Supply Chain Uncertainty
summary: Industrious Labs says a new report projects U.S. primary aluminum demand could rise by as much as 40% by 2035. The report frames this as a supply-chain and trade-policy issue, highlighting pressure on domestic production capacity.

title: New filter captures and recycles aluminum from manufacturing waste
summary: MIT engineers developed a nanofiltration process designed to reduce hazardous waste from aluminum production by capturing aluminum ions from plant effluent. In lab-scale tests, the membrane recovered more than 99% of aluminum ions and could help increase yield while reducing waste.

title: U.S. industry exceeds steel capacity utilization level outlined in Section 232 report
summary: This article says U.S. steel capacity utilization has risen from 76% last April to above 80% last week, according to American Iron and Steel Institute data. It frames the increase as a threshold relevant to the Section 232 report’s capacity-utilization level.

title: Overcapacity will continue to weigh on steel industry
summary: The piece reports that global steel overcapacity is expected to exceed 560 million tonnes this year, underscoring persistent pressure on the industry. It also notes MEPS’s forecast that global crude steel production will decline year on year in 2024.

title: Industry Data
summary: AISI’s weekly data shows domestic raw steel production at 1,877,000 net tons and capability utilization at 81.3% for the week ending June 6, 2026. This is the most recent capacity-related industry data in the results.

title: 3 innovations in green steel
summary: This article highlights recent green steel developments, including Boston Metal’s CO2-free steel process, Sweden’s planned hydrogen steel plant, and Kobe Steel’s lower-coke blast furnace technique. It also explains how hydrogen and molten oxide electrolysis could reduce or eliminate process emissions in steelmaking.

title: Smelting Barriers: The Innovators Forging a New Future for Iron and Steel
summary: RMI surveys emerging steel innovations focused on cutting emissions through electrification, hydrogen-based production, and new process designs. It also points to companies developing low-carbon solutions across the steel value chain, including waste valorization and product-life extensions.

title: Green transformation in the iron and steel industry in India
summary: This paper examines decarbonization pathways in India’s steel sector and notes that new technologies are significantly reducing emissions. It also describes active R&D by major Indian steel companies and the growing role of sustainability-focused executive commitments.

title: Copper prices are surging amid geopolitical tensions, supply shortages and AI demand
summary: The article says copper prices are rising sharply, driven by a combination of geopolitical tensions, structural supply shortages, and strong demand tied to AI infrastructure. It also shows live commodity data for copper in India, indicating an elevated spot price and open interest levels.

title: Copper prices hit a new record high, but is the metals trade 'stretched'?
summary: The clip reports that copper continued to climb after breaking above $13,000 a ton for the first time. It frames the move as a record run and raises the question of whether metals trading has become overextended.

title: Copper futures rose above $6.5 per pound, edging back toward record highs
summary: Trading Economics reports that copper futures moved above $6.5 per pound as risk appetite improved. The article links the move to reports that the US and Iran reached an agreement, which helped support broader market sentiment.

title: US miners eye copper comeback as demand rises
summary: This article says U.S. copper demand is expected to double over the next 10 years, but domestic production is not currently enough to meet that growth. It highlights the need for steps to ease supply constraints and expand capacity.

summary: Nickel prices increased by 0.98% on June 15, 2026, reaching $17,964.50 per ton. Despite this daily gain, the metal's price has fallen 3.18% over the past month, though it remains 19.25% higher than its low point.

title: World's Biggest Nickel Mine in Indonesia Told to Slash Output
summary: Bloomberg reports that PT Weda Bay Nickel, described as the world’s biggest nickel mine, has been told to drastically cut output as Indonesia seeks to support global prices. The report says the mine may receive 12 million tons of ore production quota this year, down from 42 million tons in 2025, and that the company plans to request a revision.

title: Indonesian smelters cut production on lower nickel ore quota
summary: Indonesia’s energy ministry has cut the 2026 nickel ore production quota to 260-270 million metric tons, below last year’s 320 million tons of production. The article says the reduction reflects prior oversupply and weak prices, and that the quota is also below the demand estimate from Indonesia’s nickel industry association.

title: Indonesia navigates nickel market with output cuts, policy shifts
summary: S&P Global reports that Indonesia confirmed plans to cut nickel production in 2026 to support prices and government revenue. The article also says the government is reversing earlier mining quota policy to better regulate supply volatility and address environmentally harmful operations.

title: Italy's Cogne Acciai Speciali to acquire majority stake in its scrap supplier
summary: Cogne Acciai Speciali announced it has signed a contract to acquire a 65% controlling stake in Com.Steel Inox, its stainless steel scrap supplier. The deal is intended to improve operational efficiency and strengthen the company’s environmental footprint.
